title: abrotanella trilobata
intro: Abrotanella trilobata, also known as New Zealand pepperwood or pepper tree, is a small to medium-sized evergreen shrub native to New Zealand. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Abrotanella trilobata include reducing inflammation, relieving coughs and colds, and providing antimicrobial protection. The herbal preparations of Abrotanella trilobata include infusions, decoctions, and essential oils, which can be used to make teas, salves, and other topical applications.
